research_findings: Committee V2 was mostly complete, but one remaining operational seam was cross-goal review_state contamination during long autonomous sessions.
The cleanest closeout guardrail is to reset the review payload automatically when the active goal changes, while preserving the existing same-goal merge behavior.
committee_feedback: Product council wants each iteration report to describe its own decision record, not a blended history of prior goals.
Architecture council wants the reset to happen only when the active goal changes, not on every capture call.
Operator council wants the fix to remove manual cleanup work from longer autonomous runs.

research_gate.summary: Close the lightweight committee V2 loop by enforcing review_state boundaries per goal instead of relying on manual cleanup between iterations.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Close the committee V2 loop with end-to-end guardrails and plan refresh
scope_decision.why_selected: The V2 architecture is close to baseline quality, and this guardrail removes one of the remaining operational rough edges in repeated sessions.
scope_in: Reset review_state payload automatically when capture-review.py sees a new active goal.
Keep same-goal merge behavior for iterative captures within one version.
Validate goal-boundary reset behavior in validate-kit.
scope_out: Do not redesign the review_state schema again.
Do not change same-goal accumulation behavior.
stop_conditions: Stop if the reset discards updates for the same active goal.
dissent: Keep the reset tied strictly to goal identity so review capture remains predictable.
scope_decision.next_action: Use the cleaned-up baseline to finish session ergonomics and final documentation refresh.

critique: This closes a real long-run gap without changing the visible protocol shape.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep reset behavior limited to goal changes.

review_state.goal_title: Close the committee V2 loop with end-to-end guardrails and plan refresh

results.name: validate-kit

history.goal: Add a real repository validation script and use it as the publish gate
Fix publish ordering so loop state and backlog updates are committed in the same version
Add a reference that defines what high-quality project data means for the loop
Add a structured schema for project data snapshots and evidence
Add a script that collects first-pass project data into a snapshot file
Add a script that scores whether the collected project data is good enough to act on
Integrate data snapshot and quality state into the loop's durable state model
Extend reports to include evidence sources and data quality observations
Improve next-goal selection using data quality gaps and evidence gaps
Align wrappers, protocol, README, and examples around high-quality project data acquisition
Integrate the committee-driven loop model across config, scripts, templates, and references
Persist research and committee review conclusions into loop state
Read committee review state back into project data and reports
Add a project installation script for the committee-driven kit
Require recorded review state before reporting and publication
Define lightweight councils, secretariat, and evaluator roster in config
Render council responsibilities and output contracts from config
Add a backward-compatible review-state schema for councils and scope decisions
Teach capture-review.py to persist structured research-gate results
Capture council-specific feedback summaries instead of one flat committee list
Persist selected goal, scope boundaries, assumptions, and required validation as a structured scope decision
Add a lightweight evaluator rubric reference and config hook
Allow capture-review.py to persist evaluator scores and pass-revise-fail outcome
Render structured scope decisions, dissent, and evaluation outcome in reports
Expose structured review-state and evaluator readiness in project-data snapshots
Require a passing evaluator result before implementation begins when configured
Extend validate-kit to exercise the structured committee V2 flow
Rewrite committee guidance around the lighter V2 architecture
Allow capture-review.py to persist escalation status and recommended action
Block goal selection when research or data quality says more context is required
Add a lightweight evaluator brief script driven by scope decision and rubric
Model escalation policy for repeated review or validation failures
Surface stop conditions, open gaps, and escalation reasons in reports and docs
Align wrappers and skill docs with councils, evaluator, and readiness blocking
Close the committee V2 loop with end-to-end guardrails and plan refresh
